arm: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Armenian.
army: noun. A large, highly organized military force, concerned mainly with ground (rather than air or naval) operations.
art: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for artificial languages.
atom: noun. The smallest possible amount of matter which still retains its identity as a chemical element, now known to consist of a nucleus
atomy: noun. A tiny particle; an atom, a mote, a speck.
mar: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Marathi.
maw: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mampruli.

mayor: noun. The chief executive of the municipal government of a city, borough, etc., formerly usually appointed as a caretaker by European royal
moa: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mwan.
moat: noun. A deep, wide defensive ditch, normally filled with water, surrounding a fortified habitation.
moo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Monom.
mort: noun. Death; especially, the death of game in hunting.
mot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Barí.
motor: noun. A machine or device that converts other energy forms into mechanical energy, or imparts motion.
motorway: noun. A highway with grade-separated crossings (rather than level crossings) and designed (and only permitted) for high-speed motor-
mow: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Moi (Congo).

oat: noun. Widely cultivated cereal grass, typically Avena sativa.
ora: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Oroha.
ort: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Adivasi Odia.
ram: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Canela.

rato: noun. Alternative form of RATO (“rocket-assisted takeoff”).
raw: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rawang.
ray: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rapa.
roam: verb. To wander or travel freely and with no specific destination.
root: noun. The part of a plant, generally underground, that anchors and supports the plant body, absorbs and stores water and nutrients, and in
rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.
rota: noun. A schedule that allocates some task, responsibility or (rarely) privilege between a set of people according to a (possibly periodic)
row: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dela-Oenale.
rya: noun. A Scandinavian knotted-pile woollen rug, used as bed-cover and tapestry.
ryot: noun. A farmer or tiller of the soil.
tam: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tamil.
taro: noun. Colocasia esculenta, raised as a food primarily for its corm, which distantly resembles potato.
taw: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tai.
tom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tombulu.
too: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xicotepec de Juárez Totonac.
tor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Togbo-Vara Banda.
tory: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Tory.
tram: noun. A passenger vehicle for public use that runs on tracks in the road (called a streetcar or trolley in North America).
tray: noun. A small, typically rectangular or round, flat, and rigid object upon which things are carried.
troy: adjective. Of, or relating to, troy weight.
try: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Turung.
tryma: noun. A drupe with fleshy exocarp, dehiscent, such as the walnut.
twa: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Twana.
two: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 2.
